name: Jarvis Channel Daemon
description: New system component: Slack channel polling daemon that monitors #jarvis for messages from David, routes them to a headless Claude Code session (Jarvis master agent with full context via CLAUDE.md bootstrap), and posts responses back via Jarvis bot. Includes adaptive polling (5min idle → 30s active → progressive backoff → 5min after silence), session ID persistence for resumption across daemon restarts, automatic context compaction every 10 turns, and stale process cleanup on startup.

files.description: Main daemon: polls #jarvis Slack channel every 5 min (idle) → 30s (active). Routes messages to headless Claude Code. Posts responses via Jarvis bot. Adaptive polling, session persistence, context compaction, process cleanup.
Setup script: verifies claude binary, checks SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, creates data directory, runs test mode.
Git ignore: excludes data/*.json and data/*.log from version control.
Added jarvis-channel task entry: runs Python daemon every 5 minutes (self-managing), keeps session awake, requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.
Added JARVIS_CHANNEL_ID and JARVIS_USER_ID documentation with defaults and use case for packaging evolution.

changelog: New system component: jarvis-channel daemon monitors #jarvis Slack channel for messages from David
Routes messages to headless Claude Code session running Jarvis master agent with full context
Posts responses back as Jarvis bot using SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
Adaptive polling strategy: 5min idle, 30s-300s active with backoff, 5min after silence
Session ID persistence enables conversation continuity across daemon restarts
Automatic context compaction every 10 turns prevents context window overflow
Stale process cleanup on startup prevents orphaned sessions
Environment variables JARVIS_CHANNEL_ID and JARVIS_USER_ID enable repackaging for other IES users
Scheduled task configuration supports Cowork's native Scheduled Tasks panel
